channel.title: Follow Builders — X/Twitter
channel.description: AI builders' latest tweets, curated by follow-builders

Bio: I like to train large deep neural nets. Previously Director of AI @ Tesla, founding team @ OpenAI, PhD @ Stanford. ❤️ 2028 🔁 164

item.title: Peter Yang (@petergyang): 1/ Plan in short sprints 2/ Encourage demos and evals over doc (get a prototype in front of internal...
item.description:
1/ Plan in short sprints 2/ Encourage demos and evals over doc (get a prototype in front of internal folks and users asap!) 3/ Revisit features with new models 4/ Do the simple thing first https://t.co/ZH1EO6lJQZ
Bio: I share extremely practical AI tutorials and interviews | Join 140K+ readers at https://t.co/XYKTmGVH14 | Product at Roblox ❤️ 87 🔁 5

item.description:
Bihan Jiang (Director of Product, Decagon) and Kai Xin Tai (Senior Product Manager, Datadog) also shared how their teams get a prototype in front of a customer way faster than they used to.
Read more: https://t.co/u6G2IWCCII
Bio: claude code + cowork @anthropicai, prev: @dagster, @scale_ai ❤️ 174 🔁 11

item.title:
Cat Wu (@_catwu): 4/ Do the simple thing
With agentic systems, failures compound with system complexity. Find the sim...
item.description:
4/ Do the simple thing
With agentic systems, failures compound with system complexity. Find the simplest thing that works.
Bio: claude code + cowork @anthropicai, prev: @dagster, @scale_ai ❤️ 125 🔁 4

item.title:
Cat Wu (@_catwu): 3/ Revisit features with new models
Every model release, go back through your list of features that...
item.description:
3/ Revisit features with new models
Every model release, go back through your list of features that were too hard for the previous model and test the ideas again. Also, remove the extra scaffolding that is no longer needed.
Bio: claude code + cowork @anthropicai, prev: @dagster, @scale_ai ❤️ 123 🔁 5

item.title: Amjad Masad (@amasad): Important to note that Replit has been on the App Store for 4 years and core functionality is exactl...
item.description:
Important to note that Replit has been on the App Store for 4 years and core functionality is exactly the same: You type (or generate) code, we compile on the server, and open a webview.
Never have been in violation of any guidelines, which Apple eventually acknowledged. https://t.co/44dTBcuEQ0
Bio: ceo @replit. civilizationist ❤️ 796 🔁 40

item.title: Amjad Masad (@amasad): Imagine leaving a product requirements meeting and Replit is already building the MVP via Granola MC...
item.description:
Imagine leaving a product requirements meeting and Replit is already building the MVP via Granola MCP. https://t.co/AWN9BSjfwu
Bio: ceo @replit. civilizationist ❤️ 567 🔁 36

Understand the universe https://t.co/s5sKMU1nRJ

item.title:
Guillermo Rauch (@rauchg): Next.js 16.2 is an agent-native framework.
𝙰𝙶𝙴𝙽𝚃𝚂.𝚖𝚍 + bundled docs make your agent an expert in th...
item.description:
Next.js 16.2 is an agent-native framework.
𝙰𝙶𝙴𝙽𝚃𝚂.𝚖𝚍 + bundled docs make your agent an expert in the exact version of Next.js you’re using.
@𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕/𝚗𝚎𝚡𝚝-𝚋𝚛𝚘𝚠𝚜𝚎𝚛 gets your agent a purpose-built tool to debug & optimize your frontend.
We proved these in production at Vercel. Agents shipped optimizations that expert frontend engineers missed or struggled to write manually.
The thing I’m most excited about is that agents will be able to harness the full power of the newer advanced React and Next.js capabilities that were conceived for humans to have the best possible end-user experience.
Bio: @vercel CEO ❤️ 647 🔁 37

item.title: Aaron Levie (@levie): It is quite ridiculous how agile you have to be with your AI agent stack right now. Whatever you spe...
item.description:
It is quite ridiculous how agile you have to be with your AI agent stack right now. Whatever you spent 6 months perfecting 12 months ago probably is already out of date and you’re better off doing a reset than trying to resuscitate it architecturally.
And what’s interesting is that for every jump in progress that eliminates one part of the stack, generally a new capability becomes possible that you need to build new scaffolding for.
For instance, probably lots of RAG pipelines have had to adjust because of context windows have improved dramatically and you can now just using agentic search due to improve tool use. But that same improved tool use means you probably need to be supporting code execution with sandboxes so the agent can handle more complex work.
So one capability gets bitter lessened, and a new one opens up altogether. This is the cycle we’re going to be in for years. If you don’t have the speed and agility to deal with it, probably going to be in a tough spot.
Bio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI ❤️ 230 🔁 20

item.title: Garry Tan (@garrytan): Unc Claude Code hackers of the world, unite! https://t.co/NC7o6cFae1

item.title: Garry Tan (@garrytan): My GStack /plan-design-review can take a 4 out of 10 mediocre design or wireframe all the way up to ...
item.description:
My GStack /plan-design-review can take a 4 out of 10 mediocre design or wireframe all the way up to a 10 out of 10 visual design.
It's amazing how helpful LLMs can be when you ask: what's the rating on of scale of 0 to 10, and what can we do to get to a 10? https://t.co/gt4DW8qY1Y
Bio: President & CEO @ycombinator —Founder https://t.co/7aoJjp1iIK—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop—haters not allowed in my sauna ❤️ 145 🔁 6

item.title: Zara Zhang (@zarazhangrui): @karpathy @NoPriorsPod @AnyGenIO Check it out: https://t.co/zoJjIjDT13
Zara Zhang (@zarazhangrui): The new @karpathy podcast with @NoPriorsPod is a must-watch. I turned the most surprising insights ...
item.description:
The new @karpathy podcast with @NoPriorsPod is a must-watch. I turned the most surprising insights & anecdotes into a webpage. For each, you can easily jump to the timetamp in the video where it occurred.
Made with @AnyGenIO (the most underrated AI tool out there)
Link below! https://t.co/1WXo7yZmRE
Bio: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w Substack: https://t.co/dDPEWGiuBW ❤️ 179 🔁 12

item.title: Dan Shipper (@danshipper): whatever you do, don't try Proof. i don't want the server to break again: https://t.co/JfAd0xIW6D

whatever you do, don't try Proof. i don't want the server to break again: https://t.co/JfAd0xIW6D
Bio: ceo @every | the only subscription you need to stay at the edge of AI ❤️ 13 🔁 0

item.title:
Dan Shipper (@danshipper): Last week our vibe-coded agent-native document editor, Proof, went viral.
4,000+ documents were cr...
item.description:
Last week our vibe-coded agent-native document editor, Proof, went viral.
4,000+ documents were created in the first two days—and then the app started crashing uncontrollably.
I vibe coded all of Proof—so I spent the next week or so not sleeping and watching Codex agents debug a codebase I barely understood.
Here's what I learned—and what it means for the future of programming: https://t.co/BUE6FBZirC
Bio: ceo @every | the only subscription you need to stay at the edge of AI ❤️ 169 🔁 12

item.title: Dan Shipper (@danshipper): it's tax season! aka send anything from your accountant straight to Codex season

item.title:
Aditya Agarwal (@adityaag): If you're finding conviction, you need to be deeply intentional.
Spend time on something that actu...
item.description:
If you're finding conviction, you need to be deeply intentional.
Spend time on something that actually matters.
If you want to build something world-class, you have to demand excellence.
@drfeifei embodies all of this, and more. Thanks for coming by @southpkcommons. https://t.co/fjwbV8mBTM
Bio: General Partner @SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder @Bevel_Health | Ex: Early Eng @facebook, CTO @Dropbox, Board @Flipkart | Optimist, Builder, Dad ❤️ 83 🔁 7

item.title:
Claude (@claudeai): Projects are now available in Cowork.
Keep your tasks and context in one place, focused on one area...
item.description:
Projects are now available in Cowork.
Keep your tasks and context in one place, focused on one area of work. Files and instructions stay on your computer.
Import existing projects in one click, or start fresh. https://t.co/4AxvfiHhiv
Bio: Claude is an AI assistant built by @anthropicai to be safe, accurate, and secure. Talk to Claude on https://t.co/ZhTwG8dz3D or download the app. ❤️ 11322 🔁 881
